   445→    /**
   446→     * Create VulnerabilityException from approved request.
   447→     *
   448→     * Logic:
   449→     * - SINGLE_VULNERABILITY scope → Create ASSET-type exception (asset_id = request.vulnerability.asset.id)
   450→     * - CVE_PATTERN scope → Create PRODUCT-type exception (target_value = request.vulnerability.cveId)
   451→     *
   452→     * @param request The approved exception request
   453→     */
   454→    private fun createExceptionFromRequest(request: VulnerabilityExceptionRequest) {
   455→        if (request.vulnerability == null) {
   456→            logger.warn("Cannot create exception: vulnerability is null for requestId={}", request.id)
   457→            return
   458→        }
   459→
   460→        val exception = when (request.scope) {
   461→            ExceptionScope.SINGLE_VULNERABILITY -> {
   462→                // Create ASSET-type exception for this specific asset
   463→                VulnerabilityException(
   464→                    exceptionType = VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType.ASSET,
   465→                    targetValue = request.vulnerability!!.asset.name, // Asset name for display
   466→                    assetId = request.vulnerability!!.asset.id,
   467→                    expirationDate = request.expirationDate,
   468→                    reason = request.reason,
   469→                    createdBy = request.reviewedByUsername ?: request.requestedByUsername
   470→                )
   471→            }
   472→            ExceptionScope.CVE_PATTERN -> {
   473→                // Create PRODUCT-type exception matching CVE pattern across all assets
   474→                VulnerabilityException(
   475→                    exceptionType = VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType.PRODUCT,
   476→                    targetValue = request.vulnerability!!.vulnerabilityId ?: "UNKNOWN_CVE",
   477→                    expirationDate = request.expirationDate,
   478→                    reason = request.reason,
   479→                    createdBy = request.reviewedByUsername ?: request.requestedByUsername
   480→                )
   481→            }
   482→        }
   483→
   484→        vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.save(exception)
   485→
   486→        logger.info(
   487→            "Created VulnerabilityException from request: requestId={}, exceptionId={}, type={}, scope={}",
   488→            request.id, exception.id, exception.exceptionType, request.scope
   489→        )
   490→    }
   491→
   492→    /**
   493→     * Delete VulnerabilityException associated with a request (for auto-approval revocation).
   494→     *
   495→     * Logic:
   496→     * - SINGLE_VULNERABILITY scope → Delete ASSET-type exception matching asset + expiration + reason
   497→     * - CVE_PATTERN scope → Delete PRODUCT-type exception matching CVE + expiration + reason
   498→     *
   499→     * @param request The exception request to delete exceptions for
   500→     */
   501→    private fun deleteExceptionForRequest(request: VulnerabilityExceptionRequest) {
   502→        if (request.vulnerability == null) {
   503→            logger.warn("Cannot delete exceptions: vulnerability is null for requestId={}", request.id)
   504→            return
   505→        }
   506→
   507→        try {
   508→            when (request.scope) {
   509→                ExceptionScope.SINGLE_VULNERABILITY -> {
   510→                    // Find ASSET-type exceptions matching this asset
   511→                    val assetId = request.vulnerability!!.asset.id
   512→                    val exceptions = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExceptionTypeAndAssetId(
   513→                        VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType.ASSET,
   514→                        assetId!!
   515→                    )
   516→
   517→                    for (exception in exceptions) {
   518→                        // Check if this exception was created for this request
   519→                        // (matches expiration date and reason)
   520→                        if (exception.expirationDate == request.expirationDate &&
   521→                            exception.reason == request.reason
   522→                        ) {
   523→                            vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.delete(exception)
   524→                            logger.debug("Deleted ASSET exception {} for asset {}", exception.id, assetId)
   525→                        }
   526→                    }
   527→                }
   528→
   529→                ExceptionScope.CVE_PATTERN -> {
   530→                    // Find PRODUCT-type exceptions matching this CVE
   531→                    val cveId = request.vulnerability!!.vulnerabilityId ?: return
   532→                    val exceptions = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExceptionTypeAndTargetValue(
   533→                        VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType.PRODUCT,
   534→                        cveId
   535→                    )
   536→
   537→                    for (exception in exceptions) {
   538→                        // Check if this exception was created for this request
   539→                        if (exception.expirationDate == request.expirationDate &&
   540→                            exception.reason == request.reason
   541→                        ) {
   542→                            vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.delete(exception)
   543→                            logger.debug("Deleted PRODUCT exception {} for CVE {}", exception.id, cveId)
   544→                        }
   545→                    }
   546→                }
   547→            }
   548→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   549→            logger.error("Error deleting exceptions for request {}: {}", request.id, e.message, e)
   550→            throw IllegalStateException("Failed to delete associated exception: ${e.message}")
   551→        }
   552→    }
   553→
   554→    /**
   555→     * Check for duplicate active requests (PENDING or APPROVED) for the same vulnerability.
   556→     *
   557→     * @param vulnerabilityId ID of the vulnerability
   558→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if active request already exists
   559→     */
   560→    private fun checkForDuplicateActiveRequest(vulnerabilityId: Long) {
   561→        val activeStatuses = listOf(ExceptionRequestStatus.PENDING, ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED)
   562→        val existingRequests = requestRepository.findByVulnerabilityIdAndStatusIn(vulnerabilityId, activeStatuses)
   563→
   564→        for (existing in existingRequests) {
   565→            if (existing.status == ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED) {
   566→                // Verify the actual exception still exists — it may have been deleted by admin
   567→                if (!hasMatchingException(existing)) {
   568→                    logger.info("Auto-cancelling orphaned APPROVED request id={} (exception was deleted)", existing.id)
   569→                    existing.status = ExceptionRequestStatus.CANCELLED
   570→                    requestRepository.update(existing)
   571→                    continue
   572→                }
   573→            }
   574→            // PENDING requests or APPROVED with existing exception → block
   575→            throw IllegalArgumentException(
   576→                "This vulnerability already has an active exception request " +
   577→                "(Status: ${existing.status}, Expires: ${existing.expirationDate}). " +
   578→                "You cannot request another exception until the current one expires or is cancelled."
   579→            )
   580→        }
   581→    }
   582→
   583→    /**
   584→     * Check whether a matching VulnerabilityException still exists for an APPROVED request.
   585→     *
   586→     * Uses the same matching logic as [deleteExceptionForRequest]: match by exception type,
   587→     * asset/CVE, expiration date, and reason.
   588→     *
   589→     * @param request The APPROVED exception request to check
   590→     * @return true if a matching exception exists, false if it was deleted
   591→     */
   592→    private fun hasMatchingException(request: VulnerabilityExceptionRequest): Boolean {
   593→        if (request.vulnerability == null) return false
   594→
   595→        return when (request.scope) {
   596→            ExceptionScope.SINGLE_VULNERABILITY -> {
   597→                val assetId = request.vulnerability!!.asset.id ?: return false
   598→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExceptionTypeAndAssetId(
   599→                    VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType.ASSET, assetId
   600→                ).any { it.expirationDate == request.expirationDate && it.reason == request.reason }
   601→            }
   602→            ExceptionScope.CVE_PATTERN -> {
   603→                val cveId = request.vulnerability!!.vulnerabilityId ?: return false
   604→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExceptionTypeAndTargetValue(
   605→                    VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType.PRODUCT, cveId
   606→                ).any { it.expirationDate == request.expirationDate && it.reason == request.reason }
   607→            }
   608→        }
   609→    }
   610→
   611→    /**
   612→     * Handle optimistic lock failure by fetching current state and throwing ConcurrentApprovalException.
   613→     *
   614→     * @param requestId ID of the request
   615→     * @param action Action that was attempted ("approve" or "reject")
   616→     * @throws ConcurrentApprovalException Always throws with current state details
   617→     */
   618→    private fun handleOptimisticLockFailure(requestId: Long, action: String): Nothing {
   619→        // Fetch current state to inform user who won the race
   620→        val currentState = requestRepository.findById(requestId).orElseThrow {
   621→            IllegalStateException("Request $requestId not found after optimistic lock failure")
   622→        }
   623→
   624→        logger.warn(
   625→            "Concurrent approval conflict on request {}: attempted to {}, but already {} by {} at {}",
   626→            requestId, action, currentState.status, currentState.reviewedByUsername, currentState.reviewDate
   627→        )
   628→
   629→        throw ConcurrentApprovalException(
   630→            reviewedBy = currentState.reviewedByUsername ?: "Unknown",
   631→            reviewedAt = currentState.reviewDate ?: LocalDateTime.now(),
   632→            currentStatus = currentState.status,
   633→            requestId = requestId
   634→        )
   635→    }
   636→
   637→    /**
   638→     * Publish ExceptionCountChangedEvent for real-time badge updates.
   639→     *
   640→     * Queries current pending count and publishes event to SSE subscribers.
   641→     * Called after operations that change pending count (create, approve, reject, cancel).
   642→     *
   643→     * **Phase 6: Real-Time Badge Updates**
   644→     */
   645→    private fun publishCountChangeEvent() {
   646→        val currentCount = requestRepository.countByStatus(ExceptionRequestStatus.PENDING)
   647→        val event = ExceptionCountChangedEvent(currentCount, this)
   648→        eventPublisher.publishEvent(event)
   649→        logger.debug("Published ExceptionCountChangedEvent: pendingCount={}", currentCount)
   650→    }
   651→
   652→    /**
   653→     * Sanitize user input to prevent XSS attacks.
   654→     *
   655→     * Removes HTML tags and normalizes whitespace.
   656→     * Prevents XSS by stripping potentially dangerous content.
   657→     *
   658→     * @param input Raw user input
   659→     * @return Sanitized text safe for storage and display
   660→     */
   661→    private fun sanitizeInput(input: String): String {
   662→        // Strip HTML tags using regex
   663→        val withoutHtml = input.replace(Regex("<[^>]*>"), "")
   664→
   665→        // Normalize whitespace (but preserve line breaks for reason/comment formatting)
   666→        val normalized = withoutHtml
   667→            .replace(Regex("\\r\\n|\\r"), "\n")  // Normalize line endings
   668→            .replace(Regex(" +"), " ")  // Collapse multiple spaces
   669→            .trim()
   670→
   671→        return normalized
   672→    }
   673→
   674→    /**
   675→     * Convert entity to DTO.
   676→     *
   677→     * @param request The exception request entity
   678→     * @return Request DTO with embedded vulnerability info
   679→     */
   680→    private fun toDto(request: VulnerabilityExceptionRequest): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
   681→        return VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto(
   682→            id = request.id!!,
   683→            vulnerabilityId = request.vulnerability?.id,
   684→            vulnerabilityCve = request.vulnerability?.vulnerabilityId,
   685→            assetName = request.vulnerability?.asset?.name,
   686→            assetIp = request.vulnerability?.asset?.ip,
   687→            requestedByUsername = request.requestedByUsername,
   688→            scope = request.scope,
   689→            reason = request.reason,
   690→            expirationDate = request.expirationDate,
   691→            status = request.status,
   692→            autoApproved = request.autoApproved,
   693→            reviewedByUsername = request.reviewedByUsername,
   694→            reviewDate = request.reviewDate,
   695→            reviewComment = request.reviewComment,
   696→            createdAt = request.createdAt!!,
   697→            updatedAt = request.updatedAt!!
   698→        )
   699→    }
   700→}
   701→

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.AwsAccountSharing
     4→import com.secman.dto.AwsAccountSharingResponse
     5→import com.secman.dto.CreateAwsAccountSharingRequest
     6→import com.secman.dto.toResponse
     7→import com.secman.repository.AwsAccountSharingRepository
     8→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
     9→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
    10→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    11→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    12→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Service for managing AWS Account Sharing rules.
    16→ *
    17→ * Feature: AWS Account Sharing
    18→ *
    19→ * Provides business logic for creating, listing, and deleting sharing rules,
    20→ * as well as resolving shared AWS account IDs for access control.
    21→ */
    22→@Singleton
    23→open class AwsAccountSharingService(
    24→    private val awsAccountSharingRepository: AwsAccountSharingRepository,
    25→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    26→    private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository
    27→) {
    28→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AwsAccountSharingService::class.java)
    29→
    30→    /**
    31→     * List all sharing rules with source/target user details and shared account counts.
    32→     * Manually paginates the eager-fetched results to avoid N+1 and LAZY issues.
    33→     */
    34→    fun listSharingRules(page: Int, size: Int): Map<String, Any> {
    35→        val allRules = awsAccountSharingRepository.findAllWithUsers()
    36→        val totalElements = allRules.size.toLong()
    37→        val totalPages = if (totalElements == 0L) 0 else ((totalElements + size - 1) / size).toInt()
    38→
    39→        val pagedRules = allRules
    40→            .drop(page * size)
    41→            .take(size)
    42→            .map { sharing ->
    43→                val count = userMappingRepository.findDistinctAwsAccountIdByEmail(sharing.sourceUser.email).size
    44→                sharing.toResponse(sharedAwsAccountCount = count)
    45→            }
    46→
    47→        return mapOf(
    48→            "content" to pagedRules,
    49→            "totalElements" to totalElements,
    50→            "totalPages" to totalPages,
    51→            "page" to page,
    52→            "size" to size
    53→        )
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    /**
    57→     * Create a new sharing rule.
    58→     *
    59→     * Validates:
    60→     * - source != target (no self-sharing)
    61→     * - Both users exist
    62→     * - No duplicate rule
    63→     * - Source user has at least one AWS account mapping
    64→     */
    65→    @Transactional
    66→    open fun createSharingRule(request: CreateAwsAccountSharingRequest, adminUserId: Long): AwsAccountSharingResponse {
    67→        if (request.sourceUserId == request.targetUserId) {
    68→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Source and target user cannot be the same")
    69→        }
    70→
    71→        val sourceUser = userRepository.findById(request.sourceUserId)
    72→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Source user not found: ${request.sourceUserId}") }
    73→        val targetUser = userRepository.findById(request.targetUserId)
    74→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Target user not found: ${request.targetUserId}") }
    75→        val adminUser = userRepository.findById(adminUserId)
    76→            .orElseThrow { IllegalStateException("Admin user not found: $adminUserId") }
    77→
    78→        if (awsAccountSharingRepository.existsBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId(
    79→                request.sourceUserId, request.targetUserId)) {
    80→            throw IllegalStateException("Sharing rule already exists from ${sourceUser.email} to ${targetUser.email}")
    81→        }
    82→
    83→        val sourceAwsAccounts = userMappingRepository.findDistinctAwsAccountIdByEmail(sourceUser.email)
    84→        if (sourceAwsAccounts.isEmpty()) {
    85→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Source user ${sourceUser.email} has no AWS account mappings to share")
    86→        }
    87→
    88→        val sharing = AwsAccountSharing(
    89→            sourceUser = sourceUser,
    90→            targetUser = targetUser,
    91→            createdBy = adminUser
    92→        )
    93→
    94→        val saved = awsAccountSharingRepository.save(sharing)
    95→        log.info("AUDIT: AWS account sharing created: source={}, target={}, admin={}, sharedAccounts={}",
    96→            sourceUser.email, targetUser.email, adminUser.email, sourceAwsAccounts.size)
    97→
    98→        return saved.toResponse(sharedAwsAccountCount = sourceAwsAccounts.size)
    99→    }
   100→
   101→    /**
   102→     * Delete a sharing rule by ID.
   103→     */
   104→    @Transactional
   105→    open fun deleteSharingRule(sharingId: Long): Boolean {
   106→        val sharing = awsAccountSharingRepository.findById(sharingId)
   107→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Sharing rule not found: $sharingId") }
   108→
   109→        awsAccountSharingRepository.delete(sharing)
   110→        log.info("AUDIT: AWS account sharing deleted: id={}, source={}, target={}",
   111→            sharingId, sharing.sourceUser.email, sharing.targetUser.email)
   112→        return true
   113→    }
   114→
   115→    /**
   116→     * Get all shared AWS account IDs for a target user.
   117→     * Used by access control services to determine asset visibility.
   118→     *
   119→     * Returns distinct AWS account IDs from all source users sharing with this target user.
   120→     */
   121→    fun getSharedAwsAccountIds(targetUserId: Long): List<String> {
   122→        return awsAccountSharingRepository.findSharedAwsAccountIdsByTargetUserId(targetUserId)
   123→    }
   124→
   125→    /**
   126→     * Get shared AWS account IDs by resolving the target user's email to their ID.
   127→     * Convenience method for services that have the email but not the user ID.
   128→     */
   129→    fun getSharedAwsAccountIdsByEmail(targetUserEmail: String): List<String> {
   130→        val user = userRepository.findByEmailIgnoreCase(targetUserEmail).orElse(null) ?: return emptyList()
   131→        return getSharedAwsAccountIds(user.id!!)
   132→    }
   133→}
   134→

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.User
     4→import com.secman.domain.UserMapping
     5→import com.secman.dto.CreateUserMappingRequest
     6→import com.secman.dto.UpdateUserMappingRequest
     7→import com.secman.dto.UserMappingResponse
     8→import com.secman.dto.toResponse
     9→import com.secman.event.UserCreatedEvent
    10→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
    11→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
    13→import io.micronaut.runtime.event.annotation.EventListener
    14→import io.micronaut.scheduling.annotation.Async
    15→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    16→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    17→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    18→import java.time.Instant
    19→
    20→@Singleton
    21→open class UserMappingService(
    22→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    23→    private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository,
    24→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    25→    private val ipAddressParser: IpAddressParser
    26→) {
    27→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(UserMappingService::class.java)
    28→    
    29→    fun getUserMappings(userId: Long): List<UserMappingResponse> {
    30→        val user = userRepository.findById(userId)
    31→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("User not found") }
    32→
    33→        val mappings = userMappingRepository.findByEmail(user.email)
    34→        return mappings.map { it.toResponse() }
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    fun getDistinctDomains(): List<String> {
    38→        val mappingDomains = userMappingRepository.findDistinctDomains()
    39→        val assetDomains = assetRepository.findDistinctAdDomains()
    40→        return (mappingDomains + assetDomains)
    41→            .map { it.lowercase() }
    42→            .distinct()
    43→            .sorted()
    44→    }
    45→    
    46→    @Transactional
    47→    open fun createMapping(userId: Long, request: CreateUserMappingRequest): UserMappingResponse {
    48→        // Validate at least one field (Feature 020: extended to include ipAddress)
    49→        if (request.awsAccountId == null && request.domain == null && request.ipAddress == null) {
    50→            throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one of Domain, AWS Account ID, or IP Address must be provided")
    51→        }
    52→
    53→        // Get user email
    54→        val user = userRepository.findById(userId)
    55→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("User not found") }
    56→
    57→        // Parse IP address if provided (Feature 020)
    58→        var ipParseResult: IpAddressParser.IpParseResult? = null
    59→        if (request.ipAddress != null) {
    60→            try {
    61→                ipParseResult = ipAddressParser.parse(request.ipAddress)
    62→            } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
    63→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid IP address format: ${e.message}", e)
    64→            }
    65→        }
    66→
    67→        // Check for duplicates (extended for IP addresses - Feature 020)
    68→        if (request.ipAddress != null) {
    69→            if (userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
    70→                    user.email, request.ipAddress, request.domain
    71→                )) {
    72→                throw IllegalStateException("IP mapping already exists for this email, IP address, and domain")
    73→            }
    74→        } else {
    75→            if (userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
    76→                    user.email, request.awsAccountId, request.domain
    77→                )) {
    78→                throw IllegalStateException("AWS mapping already exists for this email, AWS account, and domain")
    79→            }
    80→        }
    81→
    82→        // Create and save
    83→        val mapping = UserMapping(
    84→            email = user.email,
    85→            awsAccountId = request.awsAccountId,
    86→            domain = request.domain
    87→        )
    88→
    89→        // Set IP fields if IP address was provided (Feature 020)
    90→        if (ipParseResult != null) {
    91→            mapping.ipAddress = request.ipAddress
    92→            mapping.ipRangeType = ipParseResult.rangeType
    93→            mapping.ipRangeStart = ipParseResult.startIpNumeric
    94→            mapping.ipRangeEnd = ipParseResult.endIpNumeric
    95→        }
    96→
    97→        val savedMapping = userMappingRepository.save(mapping)
    98→        return savedMapping.toResponse()
    99→    }
   100→    
   101→    @Transactional
   102→    open fun updateMapping(userId: Long, mappingId: Long, request: UpdateUserMappingRequest): UserMappingResponse {
   103→        // Validate at least one field (Feature 020: extended to include ipAddress)
   104→        if (request.awsAccountId == null && request.domain == null && request.ipAddress == null) {
   105→            throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one of Domain, AWS Account ID, or IP Address must be provided")
   106→        }
   107→
   108→        // Get user
   109→        val user = userRepository.findById(userId)
   110→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("User not found") }
   111→
   112→        // Get mapping and verify ownership
   113→        val mapping = userMappingRepository.findById(mappingId)
   114→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Mapping not found") }
   115→
   116→        if (mapping.email != user.email) {
   117→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Mapping does not belong to user")
   118→        }
   119→
   120→        // Parse IP address if provided (Feature 020)
   121→        var ipParseResult: IpAddressParser.IpParseResult? = null
   122→        if (request.ipAddress != null) {
   123→            try {
   124→                ipParseResult = ipAddressParser.parse(request.ipAddress)
   125→            } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   126→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid IP address format: ${e.message}", e)
   127→            }
   128→        }
   129→
   130→        // Check for duplicates (excluding current mapping) - Feature 020
   131→        if (request.ipAddress != null) {
   132→            val isDuplicate = userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   133→                user.email, request.ipAddress, request.domain
   134→            ) && (mapping.ipAddress != request.ipAddress || mapping.domain != request.domain)
   135→
   136→            if (isDuplicate) {
   137→                throw IllegalStateException("IP mapping already exists for this email, IP address, and domain")
   138→            }
   139→        } else {
   140→            val isDuplicate = userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   141→                user.email, request.awsAccountId, request.domain
   142→            ) && (mapping.awsAccountId != request.awsAccountId || mapping.domain != request.domain)
   143→
   144→            if (isDuplicate) {
   145→                throw IllegalStateException("AWS mapping already exists for this email, AWS account, and domain")
   146→            }
   147→        }
   148→
   149→        // Update
   150→        mapping.awsAccountId = request.awsAccountId
   151→        mapping.domain = request.domain
   152→
   153→        // Update IP fields if IP address was provided (Feature 020)
   154→        if (ipParseResult != null) {
   155→            mapping.ipAddress = request.ipAddress
   156→            mapping.ipRangeType = ipParseResult.rangeType
   157→            mapping.ipRangeStart = ipParseResult.startIpNumeric
   158→            mapping.ipRangeEnd = ipParseResult.endIpNumeric
   159→        } else {
   160→            // Clear IP fields if no IP address provided
   161→            mapping.ipAddress = null
   162→            mapping.ipRangeType = null
   163→            mapping.ipRangeStart = null
   164→            mapping.ipRangeEnd = null
   165→        }
   166→
   167→        val updated = userMappingRepository.update(mapping)
   168→        return updated.toResponse()
   169→    }
   170→    
   171→    @Transactional
   172→    open fun deleteMapping(userId: Long, mappingId: Long): Boolean {
   173→        val user = userRepository.findById(userId)
   174→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("User not found") }
   175→
   176→        val mapping = userMappingRepository.findById(mappingId)
   177→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Mapping not found") }
   178→
   179→        if (mapping.email != user.email) {
   180→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Mapping does not belong to user")
   181→        }
   182→
   183→        userMappingRepository.delete(mapping)
   184→        return true
   185→    }
   186→
   187→    // Feature 042: Future User Mapping Support
   188→
   189→    /**
   190→     * Event listener for user creation - automatically applies future user mappings
   191→     *
   192→     * When a new user is created (via OAuth auto-provisioning or manual creation),
   193→     * this method checks for existing future user mappings (email-only mappings)
   194→     * and applies them to the new user account.
   195→     *
   196→     * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
   197→     *
   198→     * @param event UserCreatedEvent containing the newly created user
   199→     */
   200→    @EventListener
   201→    @Async
   202→    open fun onUserCreated(event: UserCreatedEvent) {
   203→        log.info("User created event received: email=${event.user.email}, source=${event.source}")
   204→
   205→        try {
   206→            applyFutureUserMapping(event.user)
   207→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   208→            log.error("Failed to apply future user mapping for user ${event.user.email}", e)
   209→            // Don't throw - user creation should not fail if mapping application fails
   210→        }
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    /**
   214→     * Apply future user mapping to a newly created user
   215→     *
   216→     * Looks up any existing future user mappings (mappings with matching email but no user reference)
   217→     * and applies them to the new user. Uses "pre-existing mapping wins" strategy for conflicts.
   218→     *
   219→     * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
   220→     *
   221→     * @param user The newly created user
   222→     * @return Number of mappings applied
   223→     */
   224→    @Transactional
   225→    open fun applyFutureUserMapping(user: User): Int {
   226→        var appliedCount = 0
   227→
   228→        // Find future user mappings (case-insensitive email match, no user reference, not yet applied)
   229→        val futureMappings = userMappingRepository.findByEmail(user.email)
   230→            .filter { it.user == null && it.appliedAt == null }
   231→
   232→        if (futureMappings.isEmpty()) {
   233→            log.debug("No future user mappings found for email: ${user.email}")
   234→            return 0
   235→        }
   236→
   237→        log.info("Found ${futureMappings.size} future user mapping(s) for email: ${user.email}")
   238→
   239→        for (futureMapping in futureMappings) {
   240→            // Check for conflicting pre-existing mapping (mapping with user reference for same composite key)
   241→            val hasConflict = if (futureMapping.ipAddress != null) {
   242→                userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   243→                    user.email, futureMapping.ipAddress, futureMapping.domain
   244→                ) && userMappingRepository.findByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   245→                    user.email, futureMapping.ipAddress, futureMapping.domain
   246→                ).map { it.user != null }.orElse(false)
   247→            } else {
   248→                userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   249→                    user.email, futureMapping.awsAccountId, futureMapping.domain
   250→                ) && userMappingRepository.findByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   251→                    user.email, futureMapping.awsAccountId, futureMapping.domain
   252→                ).map { it.user != null }.orElse(false)
   253→            }
   254→
   255→            if (hasConflict) {
   256→                log.warn("Pre-existing mapping conflicts with future mapping id=${futureMapping.id}, skipping application")
   257→                // Mark as applied but don't link to user (conflict resolution strategy)
   258→                futureMapping.appliedAt = Instant.now()
   259→                userMappingRepository.update(futureMapping)
   260→                continue
   261→            }
   262→
   263→            // Apply the future mapping to the user
   264→            futureMapping.user = user
   265→            futureMapping.appliedAt = Instant.now()
   266→            userMappingRepository.update(futureMapping)
   267→
   268→            log.info("Applied future user mapping id=${futureMapping.id} to user ${user.email}")
   269→            appliedCount++
   270→        }
   271→
   272→        log.info("Applied $appliedCount future user mapping(s) to user ${user.email}")
   273→        return appliedCount
   274→    }
   275→
   276→    /**
   277→     * Get current mappings (future + active) with pagination
   278→     *
   279→     * Returns all mappings that have not yet been applied (appliedAt IS NULL).
   280→     * This includes both future user mappings and active user mappings.
   281→     *
   282→     * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
   283→     *
   284→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters (page, size, sort)

    38→/**
    39→ * Controller for vulnerability management and exception operations
    40→ *
    41→ * Endpoints:
    42→ * - GET /api/vulnerabilities/current - List current vulnerabilities with filters
    43→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exceptions - List vulnerability exceptions
    44→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exceptions - Create new exception
    45→ * - PUT /api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id} - Update exception
    46→ * - DELETE /api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id} - Delete exception
    47→ *
    48→ * Access: ADMIN, VULN, and SECCHAMPION roles only
    49→ * Feature: 025-role-based-access-control
    50→ *
    51→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    52→ */
    53→@Controller
    54→@Secured("ADMIN", "VULN", "SECCHAMPION")
    55→@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
    56→open class VulnerabilityManagementController(
    57→    private val vulnerabilityService: VulnerabilityService,
    58→    private val vulnerabilityExceptionService: VulnerabilityExceptionService,
    59→    private val assetFilterService: AssetFilterService,
    60→    private val vulnerabilityTimestampMigrationService: com.secman.service.VulnerabilityTimestampMigrationService,
    61→    private val vulnerabilityExportService: VulnerabilityExportService,
    62→    private val exportJobService: ExportJobService
    63→) {
    64→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityManagementController::class.java)
    65→
    66→    @Serdeable
    67→    data class ErrorResponse(val error: String)
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Get current vulnerabilities (latest scan per asset) with exception status
    71→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
    72→     *
    73→     * GET /api/vulnerabilities/current?severity=Critical&system=server&exceptionStatus=not_excepted&product=zlib&adDomain=CONTOSO&cloudAccountId=123456789012&page=0&size=50
    74→     * Auth: ADMIN or VULN role
    75→     * Response: PaginatedVulnerabilitiesResponse
    76→     *
    77→     * FR-014, FR-016, FR-018: ADMIN sees all, VULN role respects workgroup restrictions
    78→     *
    79→     * Query Parameters:
    80→     * - severity: Optional CVSS severity filter (e.g., "Critical", "High", "Medium", "Low")
    81→     * - system: Optional system/asset name filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    82→     * - exceptionStatus: Optional filter - "excepted", "not_excepted", or omit for all
    83→     * - product: Optional product/version filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    84→     * - adDomain: Optional AD domain filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    85→     * - cloudAccountId: Optional AWS cloud account ID filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    86→     * - page: Page number (0-indexed, default: 0)
    87→     * - size: Page size (default: 50, max: 500)
    88→     * - sort: Sort field (e.g., "assetName", "cvssSeverity", "scanTimestamp", "vulnerabilityId", "vulnerableProductVersions", "daysOpen")
    89→     * - sortDir: Sort direction ("asc" or "desc", default: "desc")
    90→     */
    91→    @Get("/api/vulnerabilities/current")
    92→    @Secured("ADMIN", "VULN", "SECCHAMPION")
    93→    open fun getCurrentVulnerabilities(
    94→        authentication: Authentication,
    95→        @Nullable @QueryValue severity: String?,
    96→        @Nullable @QueryValue system: String?,
    97→        @Nullable @QueryValue exceptionStatus: String?,
    98→        @Nullable @QueryValue product: String?,
    99→        @Nullable @QueryValue adDomain: String?,
   100→        @Nullable @QueryValue cloudAccountId: String?,
   101→        @Nullable @QueryValue page: Int?,
   102→        @Nullable @QueryValue size: Int?,
   103→        @Nullable @QueryValue sort: String?,
   104→        @Nullable @QueryValue sortDir: String?
   105→    ): HttpResponse<PaginatedVulnerabilitiesResponse> {
   106→        return try {
   107→            // Validate and sanitize pagination parameters
   108→            val pageNumber = maxOf(page ?: 0, 0)
   109→            val pageSize = minOf(maxOf(size ?: 50, 1), 500)
   110→
   111→            // Default to excluding excepted vulnerabilities when no filter specified
   112→            val effectiveExceptionStatus = if (exceptionStatus.isNullOrBlank()) "not_excepted" else exceptionStatus
   113→
   114→            log.debug("Getting current vulnerabilities for user: {} - severity: {}, system: {}, exceptionStatus: {} (effective: {}), product: {}, adDomain: {}, cloudAccountId: {}, page: {}, size: {}",
   115→                authentication.name, severity, system, exceptionStatus, effectiveExceptionStatus, product, adDomain, cloudAccountId, pageNumber, pageSize)
   116→
   117→            // Check if user is ADMIN (universal access)
   118→            val isAdmin = authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
   119→
   120→            // Get accessible asset IDs (only needed for non-ADMIN users)
   121→            // PERFORMANCE: For ADMIN users, we skip this query entirely
   122→            val accessibleAssetIds = if (isAdmin) {
   123→                emptySet()  // Not used for ADMIN
   124→            } else {
   125→                assetFilterService.getAccessibleAssets(authentication)
   126→                    .mapNotNull { it.id }
   127→                    .toSet()
   128→            }
   129→
   130→            // PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZED: Use database-level pagination and access control
   131→            // This replaces the old pattern of loading all data and filtering in-memory
   132→            val response = vulnerabilityService.getCurrentVulnerabilitiesOptimized(
   133→                accessibleAssetIds = accessibleAssetIds,
   134→                isAdmin = isAdmin,
   135→                severity = severity,
   136→                system = system,
   137→                exceptionStatus = effectiveExceptionStatus,
   138→                product = product,
   139→                adDomain = adDomain,
   140→                cloudAccountId = cloudAccountId,
   141→                page = pageNumber,
   142→                size = pageSize,
   143→                sort = sort,
   144→                sortDir = sortDir
   145→            )
   146→
   147→            log.info("Returning {} current vulnerabilities for user {} (page {}/{}, total {})",
   148→                response.content.size, authentication.name, response.currentPage + 1, response.totalPages, response.totalElements)
   149→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   150→
   151→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   152→            log.error("Error fetching current vulnerabilities for user: {}", authentication.name, e)
   153→            HttpResponse.serverError()
   154→        }
   155→    }
   156→
   157→    // ==================== Export Job Endpoints ====================
   158→    // Feature: Vulnerability Export Performance Optimization - Background Job Pattern
   159→    //
   160→    // Workflow:
   161→    // 1. POST /api/vulnerabilities/export - Start export job (returns job ID)
   162→    // 2. GET /api/vulnerabilities/export/{jobId}/status - Poll for progress
   163→    // 3. GET /api/vulnerabilities/export/{jobId}/download - Download completed file
   164→    //
   165→    // Benefits:
   166→    // - No HTTP timeout issues (returns immediately)
   167→    // - Progress tracking for user feedback
   168→    // - Rate limiting (max 1 per user, 3 global)
   169→    // - Cancellation support
   170→    // - File cleanup after 24h
   171→
   172→    /**
   173→     * Start a vulnerability export job
   174→     *
   175→     * POST /api/vulnerabilities/export
   176→     * Auth: ADMIN, VULN, or SECCHAMPION role
   177→     * Response: StartExportResponse with job ID
   178→     *
   179→     * The export runs in the background. Use the status endpoint to track progress
   180→     * and the download endpoint to retrieve the file when complete.
   181→     */
   182→    @Post("/api/vulnerabilities/export")
   183→    @Secured("ADMIN", "VULN", "SECCHAMPION")
   184→    open fun startExport(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   185→        return try {
   186→            log.info("Starting vulnerability export job for user: {}", authentication.name)
   187→
   188→            val jobDto = exportJobService.startExport(authentication)
   189→
   190→            log.info("Export job created: {} for user {}", jobDto.jobId, authentication.name)
   191→            HttpResponse.status<StartExportResponse>(HttpStatus.ACCEPTED)
   192→                .body(StartExportResponse(
   193→                    jobId = jobDto.jobId,
   194→                    message = "Export job started. Poll the status endpoint for progress.",
   195→                    status = jobDto.status
   196→                ))
   197→
   198→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   199→            log.warn("Export rate limit exceeded for user {}: {}", authentication.name, e.message)
   200→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)
   201→                .body(ErrorResponse(e.message ?: "Rate limit exceeded"))
   202→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   203→            log.error("Error starting export job for user: {}", authentication.name, e)
   204→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   205→                .body(ErrorResponse("Error starting export: ${e.message}"))
   206→        }
   207→    }
   208→
   209→    /**
   210→     * Get export job status
   211→     *
   212→     * GET /api/vulnerabilities/export/{jobId}/status
   213→     * Auth: ADMIN, VULN, or SECCHAMPION role
   214→     * Response: ExportJobDto with progress information
   215→     *
   216→     * Poll this endpoint to track export progress.
   217→     * Frontend should poll every 2 seconds while job is running.
   218→     */
   219→    @Get("/api/vulnerabilities/export/{jobId}/status")
   220→    @Secured("ADMIN", "VULN", "SECCHAMPION")
   221→    open fun getExportStatus(
   222→        jobId: String,
   223→        authentication: Authentication
   224→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   225→        return try {
   226→            val jobDto = exportJobService.getJobStatus(jobId, authentication.name)
   227→
   228→            if (jobDto == null) {
   229→                log.warn("Export job not found: {} for user {}", jobId, authentication.name)
   230→                return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Export job not found"))
   231→            }
   232→
   233→            HttpResponse.ok(jobDto)
   234→
   235→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   236→            log.error("Error getting export status for job: {}", jobId, e)
   237→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   238→                .body(ErrorResponse("Error getting status: ${e.message}"))
   239→        }
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    /**
   243→     * Download completed export file
   244→     *
   245→     * GET /api/vulnerabilities/export/{jobId}/download
   246→     * Auth: ADMIN, VULN, or SECCHAMPION role
   247→     * Response: Binary Excel file
   248→     *
   249→     * Only available when job status is COMPLETED.
   250→     * Files are automatically cleaned up after 24 hours.
   251→     */
   252→    @Get("/api/vulnerabilities/export/{jobId}/download")
   253→    @Produces("application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet")
   254→    @Secured("ADMIN", "VULN", "SECCHAMPION")
   255→    open fun downloadExport(
   256→        jobId: String,
   257→        authentication: Authentication
   258→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   259→        return try {
   260→            val jobDto = exportJobService.getJobStatus(jobId, authentication.name)
   261→
   262→            if (jobDto == null) {
   263→                log.warn("Export job not found for download: {} for user {}", jobId, authentication.name)
   264→                return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Export job not found"))
   265→            }
   266→
   267→            if (!jobDto.isDownloadable) {
   268→                log.warn("Export job {} not downloadable, status: {}", jobId, jobDto.status)
   269→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   270→                    when (jobDto.status) {
   271→                        ExportJobStatus.PENDING, ExportJobStatus.PROCESSING ->
   272→                            "Export is still in progress (${jobDto.progressPercent}%)"
   273→                        ExportJobStatus.FAILED ->
   274→                            "Export failed: ${jobDto.errorMessage}"
   275→                        ExportJobStatus.CANCELLED ->
   276→                            "Export was cancelled"
   277→                        ExportJobStatus.EXPIRED ->
   278→                            "Export file has expired. Please start a new export."
   279→                        else -> "Export is not available for download"
   280→                    }
   281→                ))
   282→            }
   283→
   284→            val file = exportJobService.getExportFile(jobId, authentication.name)
   285→            if (file == null) {
   286→                log.error("Export file not found for job: {}", jobId)
   287→                return HttpResponse.serverError(ErrorResponse("Export file not found"))
   288→            }
   289→
   290→            log.info("Serving export file for job {}: {} ({} bytes)",
   291→                jobId, jobDto.fileName, jobDto.fileSizeBytes)
   292→
   293→            HttpResponse.ok(StreamedFile(
   294→                FileInputStream(file),
   295→                MediaType.of("application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet")
   296→            ).attach(jobDto.fileName ?: "vulnerabilities_export.xlsx"))
   297→
   298→        } catch (e: Exception) {

   583→    /**
   584→     * DTO for exception impact preview request
   585→     * Feature 021 Phase 3
   586→     */
   587→    @Serdeable
   588→    data class PreviewExceptionImpactRequest(
   589→        val exceptionType: ExceptionType,
   590→        val targetValue: String,
   591→        val assetId: Long? = null
   592→    )
   593→
   594→    /**
   595→     * DTO for exception impact preview response
   596→     * Feature 021 Phase 3
   597→     */
   598→    @Serdeable
   599→    data class ExceptionImpactPreview(
   600→        val affectedVulnerabilities: List<VulnerabilityWithExceptionDto>,
   601→        val totalCount: Int,
   602→        val sampleSize: Int,
   603→        val countBySeverity: Map<String, Int>
   604→    )
   605→
   606→    /**
   607→     * Preview the impact of creating an exception
   608→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Product Exception Enhancement
   609→     *
   610→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exceptions/preview
   611→     * Auth: ADMIN or VULN role
   612→     * Request: PreviewExceptionImpactRequest
   613→     * Response: ExceptionImpactPreview
   614→     *
   615→     * Shows a sample of vulnerabilities that would be affected by the exception
   616→     * along with total count and severity breakdown
   617→     */
   618→    @Post("/api/vulnerability-exceptions/preview")
   619→    open fun previewExceptionImpact(
   620→        @Valid @Body request: PreviewExceptionImpactRequest
   621→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   622→        return try {
   623→            log.debug("Previewing exception impact - type: {}, target: {}, assetId: {}",
   624→                request.exceptionType, request.targetValue, request.assetId)
   625→
   626→            val (sample, totalCount) = vulnerabilityService.previewExceptionImpact(
   627→                exceptionType = request.exceptionType,
   628→                targetValue = request.targetValue,
   629→                assetId = request.assetId,
   630→                limit = 10  // Show first 10 as sample
   631→            )
   632→
   633→            // Count by severity
   634→            val severityCounts = sample.groupingBy { it.cvssSeverity ?: "Unknown" }.eachCount()
   635→
   636→            val preview = ExceptionImpactPreview(
   637→                affectedVulnerabilities = sample,
   638→                totalCount = totalCount,
   639→                sampleSize = sample.size,
   640→                countBySeverity = severityCounts
   641→            )
   642→
   643→            log.info("Exception would affect {} total vulnerabilities (showing {} sample)",
   644→                totalCount, sample.size)
   645→            HttpResponse.ok(preview)
   646→
   647→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   648→            log.warn("Invalid preview request: {}", e.message)
   649→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(e.message ?: "Invalid request"))
   650→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   651→            log.error("Error previewing exception impact", e)
   652→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   653→                .body(ErrorResponse("Error previewing impact: ${e.message}"))
   654→        }
   655→    }

   657→    /**
   658→     * Cleanup duplicate vulnerabilities
   659→     *
   660→     * POST /api/vulnerabilities/cleanup-duplicates
   661→     * Auth: ADMIN role ONLY
   662→     * Response: VulnerabilityCleanupResult with statistics
   663→     *
   664→     * Removes duplicate vulnerabilities from the database. A duplicate is defined as:
   665→     * - Same asset ID
   666→     * - Same vulnerability ID (CVE)
   667→     * - Same scan timestamp
   668→     *
   669→     * Keeps the oldest record (lowest ID) and deletes the rest.
   670→     * The operation is idempotent and can be safely called multiple times.
   671→     *
   672→     * Feature: Vulnerability Duplicate Cleanup
   673→     * User Story: As an ADMIN, I want to cleanup duplicate vulnerabilities
   674→     *             so that the database remains clean and reporting is accurate
   675→     */
   676→    @Post("/api/vulnerabilities/cleanup-duplicates")
   677→    @Secured("ADMIN")  // ADMIN only
   678→    open fun cleanupDuplicates(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   679→        return try {
   680→            log.info("Cleanup duplicates requested by user: {}", authentication.name)
   681→
   682→            val result = vulnerabilityService.cleanupDuplicates()
   683→
   684→            log.info("Cleanup completed: duplicatesRemoved={}, uniqueRemaining={}",
   685→                result.duplicatesRemoved, result.uniqueVulnerabilitiesRemaining)
   686→
   687→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
   688→
   689→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   690→            log.error("Error during vulnerability cleanup", e)
   691→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   692→                .body(ErrorResponse("Error during cleanup: ${e.message}"))
   693→        }
   694→    }

   696→    /**
   697→     * Migrate vulnerability timestamps from import time to discovery date
   698→     *
   699→     * POST /api/vulnerabilities/migrate-timestamps?dryRun=true
   700→     * Auth: ADMIN role ONLY
   701→     * Response: MigrationResult with statistics
   702→     *
   703→     * Fixes the Feature 048 bug where scanTimestamp was set to import execution time
   704→     * instead of vulnerability discovery date. This caused overdue status calculation errors
   705→     * (e.g., 901-day-old vulnerabilities showing as "OK" because age was calculated from
   706→     * last import time, not discovery date).
   707→     *
   708→     * The migration:
   709→     * 1. Fetches all vulnerabilities
   710→     * 2. Parses daysOpen field (e.g., "901 days" → 901)
   711→     * 3. Calculates discovery timestamp: currentTime - daysOpen
   712→     * 4. Updates scanTimestamp to the calculated discovery date
   713→     * 5. Handles patchPublicationDate if available (Feature 041)
   714→     *
   715→     * Query Parameters:
   716→     * - dryRun: If true, only logs what would be changed without saving (default: false)
   717→     *
   718→     * Feature: 048 Fix - Prevent Duplicate Vulnerabilities (Timestamp Correction)
   719→     * User Story: As an ADMIN, I want to fix existing vulnerability timestamps
   720→     *             so that overdue status calculations are accurate
   721→     */
   722→    @Post("/api/vulnerabilities/migrate-timestamps")
   723→    @Secured("ADMIN")  // ADMIN only
   724→    open fun migrateTimestamps(
   725→        authentication: Authentication,
   726→        @QueryValue(defaultValue = "false") dryRun: Boolean
   727→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   728→        return try {
   729→            log.info("Timestamp migration requested by user: {} (dryRun={})", authentication.name, dryRun)
   730→
   731→            val result = vulnerabilityTimestampMigrationService.migrateVulnerabilityTimestamps(dryRun)
   732→
   733→            log.info("Migration completed: totalProcessed={}, migrated={}, skipped={}, errors={}",
   734→                result.totalProcessed, result.migrated, result.skipped, result.errors)
   735→
   736→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
   737→
   738→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   739→            log.error("Error during vulnerability timestamp migration", e)
   740→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   741→                .body(ErrorResponse("Error during migration: ${e.message}"))
   742→        }
   743→    }
   744→
   745→    /**
   746→     * Migrate import timestamp for existing vulnerabilities
   747→     *
   748→     * Endpoint: POST /api/vulnerabilities/migrate-import-timestamp?dryRun=true
   749→     *
   750→     * Sets importTimestamp = createdAt for all vulnerabilities where importTimestamp is NULL.
   751→     * This is needed after adding the importTimestamp field to support the "current" query
   752→     * which uses MAX(importTimestamp) to find vulnerabilities from the latest import batch.
   753→     *
   754→     * Query Parameters:
   755→     * - dryRun: If true, only logs what would be changed without saving (default: false)
   756→     *
   757→     * Feature: Import Timestamp field for Current Vulnerabilities query
   758→     * User Story: As an ADMIN, I want to migrate existing vulnerabilities to have importTimestamp
   759→     *             so that the Current Vulnerabilities view shows all vulns from the latest import
   760→     */
   761→    @Post("/api/vulnerabilities/migrate-import-timestamp")
   762→    @Secured("ADMIN")  // ADMIN only
   763→    open fun migrateImportTimestamp(
   764→        authentication: Authentication,
   765→        @QueryValue(defaultValue = "false") dryRun: Boolean
   766→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   767→        return try {
   768→            log.info("ImportTimestamp migration requested by user: {} (dryRun={})", authentication.name, dryRun)
   769→
   770→            val result = vulnerabilityTimestampMigrationService.migrateImportTimestamp(dryRun)
   771→
   772→            log.info("ImportTimestamp migration completed: totalProcessed={}, migrated={}, skipped={}, errors={}",
   773→                result.totalProcessed, result.migrated, result.skipped, result.errors)
   774→
   775→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
   776→
   777→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   778→            log.error("Error during importTimestamp migration", e)
   779→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   780→                .body(ErrorResponse("Error during importTimestamp migration: ${e.message}"))
   781→        }
   782→    }

   784→    /**
   785→     * Add or update vulnerability via CLI
   786→     * Feature: 052-cli-add-vulnerability
   787→     *
   788→     * POST /api/vulnerabilities/cli-add
   789→     * Auth: ADMIN or VULN role
   790→     * Request: AddVulnerabilityRequestDto
   791→     * Response: AddVulnerabilityResponseDto (200 OK)

    25→/**
    26→ * REST API controller for vulnerability exception request workflow.
    27→ *
    28→ * Endpoints:
    29→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests - Create new request
    30→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my - Get user's own requests
    31→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my/summary - Get user's request statistics
    32→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id} - Get request by ID
    33→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve - Approve request (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION only)
    34→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/reject - Reject request (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION only)
    35→ * - DELETE /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id} - Cancel own pending request
    36→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending - Get all pending requests (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION only)
    37→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending/count - Get pending count for badge
    38→ *
    39→ * Concurrency Control: First-approver-wins using optimistic locking
    40→ * Audit Logging: All state transitions logged asynchronously
    41→ *
    42→ * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
    43→ * Reference: contracts/exception-request-api.yaml, quickstart.md
    44→ */
    45→@Controller("/api/vulnerability-exception-requests")
    46→@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
    47→open class VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController(
    48→    private val exceptionRequestService: VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService,
    49→    private val statisticsService: ExceptionRequestStatisticsService,
    50→    private val exportService: ExceptionRequestExportService
    51→) {
    52→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController::class.java)
    53→
    54→    @Serdeable
    55→    data class ErrorResponse(
    56→        val error: String,
    57→        val details: String? = null,
    58→        val status: Int
    59→    )
    60→
    61→    @Serdeable
    62→    data class PendingCountResponse(val count: Long)
    63→
    64→    /**
    65→     * TASK-016: Create a new exception request.
    66→     *
    67→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests
    68→     * Auth: All authenticated users
    69→     * Auto-approval: If user has ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role, status = APPROVED immediately
    70→     *
    71→     * @param dto Request creation data (vulnerabilityId, scope, reason, expirationDate)
    72→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
    73→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
    74→     * @return 201 Created with request DTO, 400 if validation fails, 409 if duplicate active request
    75→     */
    76→    @Post
    77→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
    78→    open fun createRequest(
    79→        @Valid @Body dto: CreateExceptionRequestDto,
    80→        authentication: Authentication,
    81→        request: HttpRequest<*>
    82→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
    83→        return try {
    84→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
    85→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
    86→
    87→            logger.debug(
    88→                "Creating exception request: userId={}, vulnerabilityId={}, scope={}",
    89→                userId, dto.vulnerabilityId, dto.scope
    90→            )
    91→
    92→            val createdRequest = exceptionRequestService.createRequest(dto, userId, clientIp)
    93→
    94→            logger.info(
    95→                "Exception request created: requestId={}, status={}, autoApproved={}",
    96→                createdRequest.id, createdRequest.status, createdRequest.autoApproved
    97→            )
    98→
    99→            HttpResponse.created(createdRequest)
   100→
   101→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   102→            logger.warn("Invalid request: {}", e.message)
   103→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   104→                error = e.message ?: "Invalid request",
   105→                status = 400
   106→            ))
   107→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   108→            logger.error("Failed to create exception request", e)
   109→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   110→        }
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    /**
   114→     * TASK-017: Get user's own exception requests with optional filtering and pagination.
   115→     *
   116→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my?status=PENDING&page=0&size=20
   117→     * Auth: All authenticated users
   118→     *
   119→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   120→     * @param status Optional status filter (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED)
   121→     * @param page Page number (0-indexed, default: 0)
   122→     * @param size Page size (default: 20, options: 20/50/100)
   123→     * @return 200 OK with paginated requests
   124→     */
   125→    @Get("/my")
   126→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   127→    open fun getUserRequests(
   128→        authentication: Authentication,
   129→        @Nullable @QueryValue status: ExceptionRequestStatus?,
   130→        @Nullable @QueryValue page: Int?,
   131→        @Nullable @QueryValue size: Int?
   132→    ): HttpResponse<PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse> {
   133→        return try {
   134→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   135→            val pageNumber = maxOf(page ?: 0, 0)
   136→            val pageSize = when (size) {
   137→                20, 50, 100 -> size
   138→                else -> 20 // Default to 20
   139→            }
   140→
   141→            logger.debug(
   142→                "Getting user requests: userId={}, status={}, page={}, size={}",
   143→                userId, status, pageNumber, pageSize
   144→            )
   145→
   146→            val pageable = Pageable.from(pageNumber, pageSize, Sort.of(Sort.Order.desc("createdAt")))
   147→            val requests = exceptionRequestService.getUserRequests(userId, status, pageable)
   148→
   149→            val wrapped = PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse(
   150→                content = requests.content,
   151→                totalElements = requests.totalSize,
   152→                totalPages = requests.totalPages
   153→            )
   154→            HttpResponse.ok(wrapped)
   155→
   156→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   157→            logger.error("Failed to get user requests", e)
   158→            HttpResponse.serverError<PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse>()
   159→        }
   160→    }

   162→    /**

   171→    @Get("/my/summary")

   181→            val summary = exceptionRequestService.getUserRequestSummary(userId)

   183→            HttpResponse.ok(summary)

   191→    /**
   192→     * TASK-019: Get exception request by ID.
   193→     *
   194→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}
   195→     * Auth: All authenticated users
   196→     * Access control: Users can only view own requests, ADMIN/SECCHAMPION can view all
   197→     *
   198→     * @param id Request ID
   199→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   200→     * @return 200 OK with request DTO, 403 if not owner (unless ADMIN/SECCHAMPION), 404 if not found
   201→     */
   202→    @Get("/{id}")
   203→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   204→    open fun getRequestById(
   205→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   206→        authentication: Authentication
   207→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   208→        return try {
   209→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   210→            val request = exceptionRequestService.getRequestById(id)
   211→
   212→            // Access control: User must own request OR have ADMIN/SECCHAMPION role
   213→            val isOwner = request.requestedByUsername == authentication.name
   214→            val isAdminOrSecChampion = hasRole(authentication, "ADMIN") || hasRole(authentication, "SECCHAMPION")
   215→
   216→            if (!isOwner && !isAdminOrSecChampion) {
   217→                logger.warn("Access denied: userId={} attempted to view request {}", userId, id)
   218→                return HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(
   219→                    ErrorResponse(
   220→                        error = "Access denied",
   221→                        details = "You can only view your own requests",
   222→                        status = 403
   223→                    )
   224→                )
   225→            }
   226→
   227→            HttpResponse.ok(request)
   228→
   229→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   230→            logger.warn("Request not found: id={}", id)
   231→            HttpResponse.notFound<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   232→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   233→            logger.error("Failed to get request by ID: id={}", id, e)
   234→            HttpResponse.serverError<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   235→        }
   236→    }

   238→    /**
   239→     * TASK-020: Approve a pending exception request.
   240→     *
   241→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve
   242→     * Auth: ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only
   243→     * Concurrency: First-approver-wins (optimistic locking)
   244→     *
   245→     * @param id Request ID
   246→     * @param reviewDto Optional review comment
   247→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user (reviewer)
   248→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
   249→     * @return 200 OK with updated request, 404 if not found, 409 if concurrent approval
   250→     */
   251→    @Post("/{id}/approve")
   252→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   253→    open fun approveRequest(
   254→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   255→        @Nullable @Body reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto?,
   256→        authentication: Authentication,
   257→        request: HttpRequest<*>
   258→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   259→        return try {
   260→            val reviewerId = getUserId(authentication)
   261→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
   262→
   263→            logger.debug("Approving request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   264→
   265→            val approvedRequest = exceptionRequestService.approveRequest(
   266→                requestId = id,
   267→                reviewerUserId = reviewerId,
   268→                reviewDto = reviewDto,
   269→                clientIp = clientIp
   270→            )
   271→
   272→            logger.info("Request approved: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   273→
   274→            HttpResponse.ok(approvedRequest)
   275→
   276→        } catch (e: ConcurrentApprovalException) {
   277→            // TASK-025: Handle concurrent approval conflicts
   278→            logger.warn("Concurrent approval conflict: requestId={}, {}", id, e.getDetailedMessage())
   279→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(
   280→                ErrorResponse(
   281→                    error = e.getUserMessage(),
   282→                    details = e.getDetailedMessage(),
   283→                    status = 409
   284→                )
   285→            )
   286→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   287→            logger.warn("Invalid approval request: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   288→            HttpResponse.notFound<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   289→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   290→            // TASK-026: Handle invalid status transitions
   291→            logger.warn("Invalid status transition: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   292→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   293→                error = "Invalid status transition",
   294→                details = e.message,
   295→                status = 400
   296→            ))
   297→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   298→            logger.error("Failed to approve request: requestId={}", id, e)
   299→            HttpResponse.serverError<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   300→        }
   301→    }
   302→
   303→    /**
   304→     * TASK-021: Reject a pending exception request.
   305→     *
   306→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/reject
   307→     * Auth: ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only
   308→     * Required: Review comment (minimum 10 characters)
   309→     *
   310→     * @param id Request ID
   311→     * @param reviewDto Review comment (required, 10-1024 characters)
   312→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user (reviewer)
   313→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
   314→     * @return 200 OK with updated request, 400 if comment missing, 404 if not found, 409 if concurrent rejection
   315→     */
   316→    @Post("/{id}/reject")
   317→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   318→    open fun rejectRequest(
   319→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   320→        @Valid @Body reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto,
   321→        authentication: Authentication,
   322→        request: HttpRequest<*>
   323→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   324→        return try {
   325→            val reviewerId = getUserId(authentication)
   326→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
   327→
   328→            logger.debug("Rejecting request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   329→
   330→            val rejectedRequest = exceptionRequestService.rejectRequest(
   331→                requestId = id,
   332→                reviewerUserId = reviewerId,
   333→                reviewDto = reviewDto,
   334→                clientIp = clientIp
   335→            )
   336→
   337→            logger.info("Request rejected: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   338→
   339→            HttpResponse.ok(rejectedRequest)
   340→
   341→        } catch (e: ConcurrentApprovalException) {
   342→            // TASK-025: Handle concurrent review conflicts
   343→            logger.warn("Concurrent rejection conflict: requestId={}, {}", id, e.getDetailedMessage())
   344→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(
   345→                ErrorResponse(
   346→                    error = e.getUserMessage(),
   347→                    details = e.getDetailedMessage(),
   348→                    status = 409
   349→                )
   350→            )
   351→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   352→            logger.warn("Invalid rejection request: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   353→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   354→                error = "Validation failed",
   355→                details = e.message,
   356→                status = 400
   357→            ))
   358→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   359→            // TASK-026: Handle invalid status transitions
   360→            logger.warn("Invalid status transition: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   361→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   362→                error = "Invalid status transition",
   363→                details = e.message,
   364→                status = 400
   365→            ))
   366→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   367→            logger.error("Failed to reject request: requestId={}", id, e)
   368→            HttpResponse.serverError<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   369→        }
   370→    }

   372→    /**
   373→     * TASK-022: Cancel own pending exception request.
   374→     *
   375→     * DELETE /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}
   376→     * Auth: All authenticated users
   377→     * Access control: Only requester can cancel, only PENDING status can be cancelled
   378→     *
   379→     * @param id Request ID
   380→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   381→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
   382→     * @return 204 No Content, 403 if not owner, 400 if not PENDING, 404 if not found
   383→     */
   384→    @Delete("/{id}")
   385→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   386→    @Status(HttpStatus.NO_CONTENT)
   387→    open fun cancelRequest(
   388→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   389→        authentication: Authentication,
   390→        request: HttpRequest<*>
   391→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   392→        return try {
   393→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   394→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
   395→
   396→            logger.debug("Cancelling request: requestId={}, userId={}", id, userId)
   397→
   398→            exceptionRequestService.cancelRequest(
   399→                requestId = id,
   400→                requesterUserId = userId,
   401→                clientIp = clientIp
   402→            )
   403→
   404→            logger.info("Request cancelled: requestId={}, userId={}", id, userId)
   405→
   406→            HttpResponse.noContent<Void>()
   407→
   408→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   409→            logger.warn("Cannot cancel request: requestId={}, userId={}, error={}", id, getUserId(authentication), e.message)
   410→            if (e.message?.contains("original requester") == true) {
   411→                HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(
   412→                    ErrorResponse(
   413→                        error = "Access denied",
   414→                        details = "Only the original requester can cancel this request",
   415→                        status = 403
   416→                    )
   417→                )
   418→            } else {
   419→                HttpResponse.notFound<ErrorResponse>()
   420→            }
   421→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   422→            // TASK-026: Handle invalid status transitions
   423→            logger.warn("Invalid cancellation: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   424→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   425→                error = "Cannot cancel this request",
   426→                details = e.message,
   427→                status = 400
   428→            ))
   429→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   430→            logger.error("Failed to cancel request: requestId={}", id, e)
   431→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   432→        }
   433→    }

   435→    /**

   438→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending?page=0&size=20

   511→    /**
   512→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/statistics
   513→     *
   514→     * Get statistical metrics for exception requests.
   515→     *
   516→     * **Access Control**: ADMIN and SECCHAMPION roles only
   517→     *
   518→     * **Query Parameters**:
   519→     * - dateRange: Optional (7days, 30days, 90days, alltime), default 30days
   520→     *
   521→     * **Response**: 200 OK with ExceptionStatisticsDto containing:
   522→     * - totalRequests: Total count in date range
   523→     * - approvalRatePercent: Approval percentage (null if no reviewed requests)
   524→     * - averageApprovalTimeHours: Median approval time in hours (null if no approved requests)
   525→     * - requestsByStatus: Map of status → count
   526→     * - topRequesters: Top 10 requesters with counts
   527→     * - topCVEs: Top 10 CVEs with counts
   528→     *
   529→     * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
   530→     * User Story 8: Analytics & Reporting (P3)
   531→     * Phase 11: Analytics & Reporting
   532→     * Reference: spec.md acceptance scenario US8-1
   533→     */
   534→    @Get("/statistics")
   535→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   536→    open fun getStatistics(
   537→        authentication: Authentication,
   538→        @QueryValue @Nullable dateRange: String?
   539→    ): HttpResponse<ExceptionStatisticsDto> {
   540→        return try {
   541→            val effectiveDateRange = dateRange ?: "30days"
   542→
   543→            logger.info("Statistics requested: dateRange={}, user={}", effectiveDateRange, authentication.name)
   544→
   545→            // Get metrics from statistics service
   546→            val approvalRate = statisticsService.getApprovalRate(effectiveDateRange)
   547→            val averageApprovalTime = statisticsService.getAverageApprovalTime(effectiveDateRange)
   548→            val requestsByStatus = statisticsService.getRequestsByStatus(effectiveDateRange)
   549→            val topRequesters = statisticsService.getTopRequesters(10, effectiveDateRange)
   550→            val topCVEs = statisticsService.getTopCVEs(10, effectiveDateRange)
   551→
   552→            // Calculate total requests
   553→            val totalRequests = requestsByStatus.values.sum()
   554→
   555→            // Convert status map to string keys for JSON serialization
   556→            val statusMap = requestsByStatus.mapKeys { it.key.name }
   557→
   558→            // Convert to DTOs
   559→            val topRequesterDtos = topRequesters.map { (username, count) ->
   560→                TopRequesterDto(username, count)
   561→            }
   562→            val topCVEDtos = topCVEs.map { (cveId, count) ->
   563→                TopCVEDto(cveId, count)
   564→            }
   565→
   566→            val statistics = ExceptionStatisticsDto(
   567→                totalRequests = totalRequests,
   568→                approvalRatePercent = approvalRate,
   569→                averageApprovalTimeHours = averageApprovalTime,
   570→                requestsByStatus = statusMap,
   571→                topRequesters = topRequesterDtos,
   572→                topCVEs = topCVEDtos
   573→            )
   574→
   575→            logger.debug("Statistics computed: totalRequests={}, approvalRate={}, avgTime={}h",
   576→                totalRequests, approvalRate, averageApprovalTime)
   577→
   578→            HttpResponse.ok(statistics)
   579→
   580→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   581→            logger.error("Failed to get statistics: dateRange={}", dateRange, e)
   582→            HttpResponse.serverError<ExceptionStatisticsDto>()
   583→        }
   584→    }
